rationale: The cited paper appears only in grouped literature-review citations, mainly as an example that functional-form assumptions or individual choice-set data can be used in consideration-set research. The citing paper does not rely on this specific paper's model, estimation procedure, or findings to build its own identification or empirical strategy. Its core framework is developed around other references (especially Goeree 2008, Draganska and Klapper 2011, and Berry-Pakes style methods), so this citation functions as background positioning rather than a distinct input.
